results.title: Tabletop Simulator Alternatives
results.content:
The Board Game Design Course

# The Board Game Design Course

## Where great games begin

## Tabletop Simulator Alternatives

Tabletop Simulator (TTS) is probably the most popular sandbox for creating and playing tabletop games online. However, it’s far from perfect. So, if you’re looking for Tabletop Simulator alternatives, there are other options available to you.

I’ve played around with a few TTS alternatives, and while I’m far from an expert on any of these platforms, I wanted to share with you some other options that you might want to consider. [...] So, there are some nuances here that aren’t ideal, but it is still better than some of the alternatives.

# Screentop.gg

Screentop.gg is a 2D tabletop gaming platform, similar to Tabletopia in many ways. It works well for simple card games and other games with few components that can be played using a top-down view.

It’s my favourite alternative to TTS and one I have used for a number of games. It’s reasonably easy to get into and has become a favourite platform at Protospiel Online.

Tabletop Simulator Alternatives 3

The biggest benefits of Screentop are that it is browser-based and free (for a limited number of games and assets). But it suffers from the same limitations as Tabletopia and many other platforms in that you can’t do any scripting or anything else complex. [...] ## Joe Slack

Hey, Vegard! That is definitely one approach. Probably not what Tabletopia would hope for though. I feel I would lose track of all the accounts if I tried to host one game per email.

## Vegard Farstad (Simplicatus Games)

Sovranti is a relatively new platform that also should be on this list. I haven’t used it but I know some Kickstarter creators have used it.

## Joe Slack

I’d love to know more about this one, what it offers, and how it differs from the other options.

## D. E. Walters

If you’re looking for a good alternative to Tabletop Simulator, try Board Game Arena (boardgamearena.com). They have a ton of games!

## Joe Slack

game's creators the game was concluded in 2001. in 1978 an institution called the Moorish Science ashram was established in the Pine Barrens near aungshat New Jersey it was four Seekers and scientists interested in alternative thought eventually The Institute for chaos studies was founded at the ashram they built a machine called the egg which could travel to an alternate dimension another key event in ARG history was the launch of the Beast developed by Microsoft to promote Steven Spielberg's AI artificial intelligence the film's poster and other promotional materials initiated Clues the ARG ran for three months fictional character Janine Salah uncovers a network of murders including her friend Evan Chan and other artificially intelligent beings the game Incorporated over 40 websites [...] # Using Alternate Reality Games for Research Based Learning - Robert A. Emmons Jr.
## Rutgers Online Learning Conference
141 subscribers

### Description
1173 views
Posted: 19 Aug 2023
"How can we engage students in what may, at first, seem like tedious processes in research driven projects? Students often find searching for and reading through academic articles and texts an arduous task. This is a necessary skill, and one that can be made exciting by using Alternate Reality Games.
